title: All must-have outcome checklist items pass the agent verifier run.
No console.error or unhandled promise rejections appear in the browser log during the agent verifier session.
Renders a visible error or feedback message when the user submits invalid input.
Zustand store (or equivalent for the selected stack) is used for all shared state across components.
Components are decomposed into units with distinct responsibilities; no single component file exceeds approximately 150 lines.
Tailwind utility classes are used throughout for styling.
Uses inline styles to apply styling instead of Tailwind utility classes.
Imports or uses an external component library such as MUI, Chakra, or Ant Design.
Manages shared application state in component-local useState rather than the designated state library.
Calls React hooks conditionally or inside loops, violating the rules of hooks.
Empty state UI is present in the source and renders when the collection is empty.
Interactive elements use semantic HTML tags such as button, input, and label rather than div or span with click handlers.
Uses non-semantic div or span elements with onClick handlers in place of button or input elements.
Spacing and sizing use Tailwind's default scale consistently; no arbitrary pixel values in className strings.
Transition or animation classes are applied to state changes such as item addition, deletion, or filter switch.
Typography has clear visual hierarchy; heading elements or larger text weights distinguish section titles from body content.
